pet.name: Mochi
pet.special_needs:
Senior dog with stiff joints. Needs short low-impact exercise, hydration monitoring, and calm
  enrichment.

age_context.summary: Mochi is in the young stage for a dog. A typical lifespan is about 10-13 years.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: Senior pets often need lower-impact exercise, more frequent comfort checks, and closer observation of appetite, hydration, and mobility. Shorter walks, joint-friendly movement, and calm enrichment are often easier to tolerate than intense activity.

retrieved_passages.title: Puppy and Kitten Routine Basics
retrieved_passages.content: Young pets benefit from short, predictable care blocks spread throughout the day. Frequent feeding, hydration checks, short play sessions, and bathroom or litter monitoring help establish routine without overwhelming the pet.

retrieved_passages.title: Puppy and Kitten Routine Basics
retrieved_passages.content: Exercise and enrichment for very young pets should stay short and positive. Puppies often do better with several brief walks or training moments instead of one long outing, and kittens benefit from short interactive play sessions that stop before exhaustion.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: Older pets may benefit from routines that include medication reminders, slow feeding, and extra time for transitions. Owners should watch for changes in gait, confusion, appetite, and bathroom habits and report major changes to a veterinarian.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Observation tasks are useful for special-needs pets because they create a record of appetite, water intake, energy, and bathroom habits. These tasks should be short, specific, and easy to complete consistently.

raw_recommendations.name: Morning comfort and mobility check
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe Mochi's gait, stiffness level, and overall comfort upon waking. Note any changes in movement or posture.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior pets benefit from close observation of mobility and gait changes. Early morning checks establish a baseline and help track joint stiffness patterns.

raw_recommendations.name: Hydration monitoring and water bowl refresh
raw_recommendations.notes: Check water bowl level, refill with fresh water, and observe whether Mochi drinks. Record intake if possible.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior pets require frequent hydration checks. Monitoring water intake helps detect early signs of health changes and ensures adequate fluid intake for joint and organ health.

raw_recommendations.name: Low-impact short walk or gentle movement
raw_recommendations.notes: Keep walk short and flat. Allow Mochi to set the pace. Stop if limping or excessive fatigue is observed. Joint-friendly movement only.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior pets with stiff joints need lower-impact exercise in shorter sessions. Multiple brief outings are better tolerated than one long walk and support mobility without overexertion.

raw_recommendations.name: Appetite and feeding observation
raw_recommendations.notes: Offer meal at consistent time. Observe whether Mochi eats normally, eats slowly, or shows reduced interest. Record any changes.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Monitoring appetite is critical for senior pets. Changes in eating habits can signal health issues. Consistent feeding times support routine and predictability.

raw_recommendations.name: Calm enrichment session
raw_recommendations.notes: Offer low-energy enrichment: puzzle toy, gentle sniff game, or quiet interaction. Stop before Mochi shows fatigue.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior pets benefit from calm enrichment that engages the mind without physical strain. Short, positive sessions prevent boredom while respecting joint limitations.

raw_recommendations.name: Evening bathroom and comfort check
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe bathroom habits and note any changes in frequency or consistency. Check for signs of discomfort or stiffness.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior pets require monitoring of bathroom habits as changes can indicate health concerns. Evening checks help track daily patterns and comfort levels.

raw_recommendations.name: Weekly joint and coat assessment
raw_recommendations.notes: Gently palpate joints, check for swelling or heat, observe coat condition and skin. Note any new lumps, sores, or behavioral changes.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Regular hands-on assessment of a senior dog's joints and overall condition helps detect early changes in mobility, comfort, or health status.

raw_recommendations.name: Monthly health summary and veterinary note preparation
raw_recommendations.notes: Review all observations from the month: mobility changes, appetite patterns, hydration, bathroom habits, energy levels, and any behavioral shifts. Prepare summary for veterinarian if needed.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Documenting monthly trends creates a health record for senior pets and helps identify patterns that warrant veterinary attention. This supports proactive care and early detection of age-related changes.
